title: Fix backend authorization for updateSiteSettings — persistent Unauthorized error

requirements.text: Audit and rewrite the backend authorization logic in backend/main.mo for the updateSiteSettings (and any other admin-protected) actor methods. The root cause is that the admin principal list is either empty at runtime, incorrectly seeded, or the caller identity is being compared against a stale/wrong value. The fix must: (1) capture the deployer's principal at canister install time (using the install message caller) and store it as the initial admin in stable state; (2) compare the runtime caller principal against this stored admin list using Principal.equal; (3) ensure the check is performed correctly so that an authenticated Internet Identity principal that was the deployer is always granted access without throwing an Unauthorized error.

quotes: Access denied — please ensure you are logged in as an admin

acceptanceCriteria: The canister records the deploying principal as admin in stable state during canister initialization (system func init or postupgrade).
Calling updateSiteSettings with the authenticated admin principal succeeds and returns the updated settings without an Unauthorized error.
Calling updateSiteSettings with an unauthenticated or non-admin principal still returns an Unauthorized error.
getSiteSettings remains publicly callable without authentication.
No regression in other admin-protected methods (addProduct, updateProduct, deleteProduct).

requirements.text: On the frontend, update the useActor hook usage in SiteSettingsSection and the updateSiteSettings mutation in useQueries.ts to ensure the authenticated actor (not the anonymous actor) is always used when calling updateSiteSettings. If the actor is not yet authenticated, the mutation should reject early and surface a clear 'Please log in to save settings' error toast rather than sending an anonymous call that the backend rejects.

quotes: Access denied — please ensure you are logged in as an admin

acceptanceCriteria: The updateSiteSettings mutation uses the authenticated actor (Internet Identity identity attached) when the user is logged in.
If the user is not authenticated, the mutation does not call the backend and instead shows a toast: 'Please log in to save settings'.
If the backend returns an Unauthorized error despite authentication, the existing descriptive error toast 'Access denied — please ensure you are logged in as an admin' is displayed.
On a successful save, a success toast is displayed.

constraints: Do not change the SiteSettings record shape or add/remove any fields.
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Preserve all existing admin-protected backend methods and their signatures.
nonGoals: Changes to the color scheme, promo banner, or Shopify integration features.
Adding new admin users beyond the deployer principal.
